response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a skilled full-stack and systems engineer with strong TypeScript/Node.js, event-driven architecture, and agile delivery experience, but the role demands deep, current C++ expertise as its core competency. His C++ exposure is limited to a brief stint at National Instruments in 2014–2015 and a bachelor thesis project; it is neither recent nor at the depth required for high-performance, multi-threaded industrial systems. He has no demonstrable experience with industrial cybersecurity (IEC 62443), MQTT, industrial protocols (Modbus, OPC UA, IEC 61850), or secure development lifecycle practices — all of which are central to this position. His career trajectory is firmly web/cloud-oriented, making him a weak fit for an on-premises industrial automation role.
strengths: Proven event-driven and distributed-systems thinking across multiple projects (GISCollective, Sociomantic microservices handling millions of HTTP requests/second)
Strong agile engineering discipline including TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, and mentoring experience (workshops, code kata sessions)
Broad polyglot background with cloud (AWS CDK), REST API design, and IoT-adjacent work that could partially transfer to the advantageous-skills section
concerns: C++ experience is minimal and over a decade old — insufficient for the role's core requirement of architecting and building high-performance C++/STL systems
No evidence of industrial cybersecurity knowledge, IEC 62443 compliance, SDL practices, or secure embedded/OT development
No exposure to industrial protocols (MQTT, Modbus TCP/IP, OPC UA, IEC 61850) or industrial automation domain context
